title: Market Brief — Saturday, May 30, 2026
summary: Compute remains severely tight on mature silicon: H200 util-avail sits at just 1%, H100 at 4%, and A100 at 7%, all well inside severe-shortage territory. B200 is the outlier at 37% util-avail with 63% of offerings idle, signaling oversupply as the market digests new capacity. The headline move is B200 spot crashing 13.3% day-over-day to $7.23/hr while H100 spiked 7.4% to $4.44/hr, a stark rotation from next-gen back into proven workhorses.
paragraphs: Compute remains severely tight on mature silicon: H200 util-avail sits at just 1%, H100 at 4%, and A100 at 7%, all well inside severe-shortage territory. B200 is the outlier at 37% util-avail with 63% of offerings idle, signaling oversupply as the market digests new capacity. The headline move is B200 spot crashing 13.3% day-over-day to $7.23/hr while H100 spiked 7.4% to $4.44/hr, a stark rotation from next-gen back into proven workhorses.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $16.24/M tokens, meaning any provider pricing 70B-class output below that is bleeding cash. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling at losses exceeding $15/M tokens, pure market-share buys. Grades: OpenAI Strong on premium tiering, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, open-source commoditized.

B200 at 37% util-avail and falling 30 points in 30 days is overvalued for spot buyers locking in contracts above $7/hr. H200 at 1% availability and $4/hr OCPI is undervalued given scarcity; go long H200 capacity agreements and short B200 spot exposure.
bullets: 1% util-avail on H200 makes it the tightest GPU class in the market today.
37% util-avail on B200 marks the only GPU in oversupply territory, down 29.9pp in 30 days.
$7.23 median B200 spot fell 13.3% in one day, the largest single-session GPU price drop this cycle.
21.78 LLMflation index means frontier inference costs have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.18 per million output tokens is the floor, set by OpenAI's open-source gpt-oss-120b via first-party API.
192.9% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI signals extreme pricing instability despite near-total utilization.
